frameworks.name: population coding with conjunction neurons
frameworks.one_line: Neural populations with conjunction responses bind features in working memory

frameworks.name: neural decoding under noise
frameworks.one_line: Swap errors and response variability emerge from decoding noisy population activity

frameworks.name: location-mediated feature binding
frameworks.one_line: Nonspatial features bind through shared location rather than directly

core_question: What neural mechanism binds different visual features into coherent objects in working memory, and how does it produce swap errors in cued recall?
key_assumptions: Object features are stored as conjunction-tuned neural population activity
Recall errors reflect decoding of noisy neural representations
Cued recall taps the same binding mechanism for spatial and nonspatial features
manipulations: Cue feature dimension (color, orientation, or location)
Similarity between items in the cue feature dimension
key_findings: Swap probability scaled with item similarity in the cue feature dimension
Swap errors in spatial and nonspatial report were strongly correlated
A conjunction-population model quantitatively fit both swap rates and response variability
interpretations: Feature binding in working memory is implemented by conjunction-tuned neural populations
Nonspatial features (color, orientation) bind exclusively via shared location, not directly to each other
